text:
Priya Shah: Acme Infra Team just confirmed they need a fully air-gapped POC next month. Key asks: offline delivery only, no outbound telemetry, upgrade windows once/month, and signed bundles. Can we outline constraints and a delivery pattern?

Acme Infra Team: correct — no egress, jumpboxes allowed, but transfers are via physical media (sneakernet). Also need a way to validate bundle provenance locally.

Ben Carter: We can do offline bundles + manifest signing. Typical flow: build -> sign with Redwood signing key (customer can validate against their supplied pubkey) -> deliver tarball or chunked delta payload. Questions: bundle size cap? and acceptance of needing an HSM/KMS for private key storage?

Acme Infra Team: prefer they keep the pubkey. We cannot hold private keys in their environment. HSM for our side is fine. Bundle size: <10GB preferable. Deltas for model weights would be h...

Iris Han: NewCo just sent a terse RFI. They need a one-page capability matrix, short answers on BYOK/HSM behavior, VPC peering, and an estimated POC quote. They want turnaround by EOD. RFI uploaded: s3://redwood-sales/newco/rfi/newco-rfi-2026.pdf

Aisha Khan: Quick thread 1025 - BalticBank wants: redacted SOC2 Type II excerpt, copy of ISO 27001 cert, and a signed pen-test attestation for the POC. They also asked if Dedicated POC can use customer-managed KMS keys and whether notarization is necessary. ETA for POC start: next week. Can we confirm process + cost? @Ethan @Omar @Clara
Ethan Park: We can produce redacted SOC2 excerpt and ISO cert copy. For KMS: customer-managed KMS on Dedicated is possible but well need to run a key-wrapping integration and VPC peering. Estimate 72-120 hours to provision.
Ravi Menon: Upload options: presigned EU-only S3 URLs (preferred), secure upload portal (one-time link), or SFTP if they insist. For EU residency we'll route to eu-central-1 bucket. Presigned example:
```
curl -T bank_evidence.zip "https://eu-files.redwood.example/upload?sig=..." -H "x-customer: baltic-123"
```

text:
Aisha Khan: Heads-up — Greenline Capital (EU) wants SOC 2 Type II excerpt + ISO 27001 certificate copy and a signed pen-test attestation for their proof-of-security pack. They also asked whether a Dedicated POC with customer-managed keys (KMS) is feasible and how that affects price. 
Ethan Park: We can hand over a redacted SOC2 summary + ISO cert copy. For KMS: customer-managed keys on Dedicated are doable but requires extra networking and key-wrapping plumbing. Will need 3-5 days for config. @Omar can you flag the pricing impact? 
Ravi Menon: Upload preference? Recommend presigned S3 URLs (short-lived) or transfer via secure upload portal (we can provide one-time links). SFTP is possible but slower to stand up. Quick sample: 
```
# client uploads
curl -T evidence.tar.gz "https://files.redwood.example/upload?sig=..." -H "x-client-id: GL123"
```

text:
Ava Chen: Heads up — Acme Health wants to procure via AWS Private Offers. Procurement team is saying invoice needs to be issued to the legal parent 'Acme Global, Inc' not the subsidiary. They're asking for the PO to reference our AWS offer SKU.
Ravi Patel: That’s common. The tricky part: AWS will bill through the marketplace seller account, which shows as 'Redwood Inference, Inc' on the AWS invoice. Procurement wants the P.O. to match the invoice entity.
Ava Chen: Right — Acme procurement said they can't process if the billing entity on the invoice != PO entity. They asked if we can route invoice to Acme Global.
Jordan Lee (Deal Desk): @Ava Chen do we have precedent? We usually: 1) recommend Private Offer with consolidated billing mapping, or 2) accept a PO to the marketplace entity and provide an intercompany addendum.
Megan Ortiz (Finance): +1. We need paper trail for revenue recogn...

Logging: we retain platform/system logs; customer is responsible for application-level logs in Private.
Marta Ruiz: to add — for Private (on-prem/VPC) we provide the control plane but customer can opt to run their own KMS/HSM. Language we usually use: "Customer-managed keys for Private deployments; Redwood-managed keys for Hosted unless BYOK is configured." Want the CAIQ ph...

text:
Ava Lin: FYI — ran into a routing gap for POCs that include multimonth commits + non-standard discounts. CPQ currently sends these to Sales Ops, but we need Deal Desk review for pricing exceptions > 15% or any legal special terms.
Ben Carter: Can you paste the CPQ field that's triggering the current route?
Ava Lin: It's this right now: `Special_Pricing_Flag__c` (boolean) + `Discount_Percent__c`. routing rule only checks flag, not commit length.
Priya Sharma: That explains it. We need a compound rule: if Commit_Months__c >= 6 OR Discount_Percent__c > 15 -> route to Deal Desk. Otherwise standard Approval Queue.
Marcus Lee: +1. Also surface `Security_Questionnaire_Required__c` — if true, force Deal Desk + Security review. Some enterprise POCs include an SSA.
Ben Carter: Agree. Proposal for new mapping:```salesforce
CASE(
 WHEN Commit_Months__c >= 6 THEN 'deal-desk'
 WHEN Discount_Percent...

text:
Maya Ortiz: hey folks — quick customer-facing Q. how do i explain “billed tokens” vs “raw tokens” in a way that doesn’t sound like we’re hiding the ball? customer is looking at their app-side token counts vs our usage export.

Maya Ortiz: also how do i describe caching? they saw a day where their prompt tokens were huge but the invoice was lower and they’re suspicious.

Stephanie Nguyen: ✅ Canonical process (SOC 2 + evidence):

text:
Stephanie Nguyen: quick PSA since this came up twice today — SOC 2 report requests should *not* be handled via email attachments / “can you just send it to me” 🙏

Stephanie Nguyen: ✅ Canonical process (SOC 2 + evidence):
1) Confirm NDA is in place (or get one executed). If they don’t have one → loop Legal via the deal room thread, don’t improvise language.
2) Open an evidence request using the playbook + ticketing flow (so GRC can track SLA + what we actually shared)
3) Only send the customer the approved link/secure share path once GRC confirms NDA + packages the artifact.

Stephanie Nguyen: Confluence playbook here (bookmark it): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/Security/soc2-customer-evidence-request-playbook

Stephanie Nguyen: TL;DR: if customer says “need SOC2 by Friday” → do NOT DM a PDF around. Create the request + tag #security-and-compliance / @Olga Petrov / @...

Avery Johnson: seeing the same security Qs pop up again this week. can we get a canonical list + where in the FAQ to point? dumping what I got from 3 prospects:
- “Do you train/fine-tune on our prompts or outputs?”
- data retention: “how long do you keep prompts/logs? can we set to 0?”
- EU residency: “can you guarantee all data stays in EU? what about support access?”
- SOC 2: “can we have the report? under NDA?”
- ISO: “are you ISO27001 certified?”
- audit logs: “what events are logged? can we export to SIEM?”
- SSO/SCIM: “okta/azure ad? does SCIM cover deprovisioning?”
- pen test: “frequency + can we see summary?”

Priyom Das: +1. Summit is asking super specifically:
- do we store full prompts in logs by default?
- can they disable logging for prod traffic?
- do we have separate retention for app logs vs audit logs?
- who are subprocessors + where’s the canonical list?

Ava Chen: Heads-up: FinEdge wants an EU-only POC w/ BYO KMS and APAC only as a disaster recovery target (no active routing). They also want a clear list of subprocessors and an egress diagram for legal. Can presales assemble a quick response + ballpark price delta by EOD?

Luca Romano: Quick Q: Which BYO KMS flavors? They mentioned 'customer-managed keys' but didn't specify provider. We can support AWS KMS in hosted via envelope encryption; Dedicated/Private supports direct customer KMS calls (AWS/GCP/Azure) or HSMs. Need to confirm.

Priya Singh: For hosted with BYO the model is: customer wraps a DEK and uploads, we store wrapped key + attest. If they require direct KMS API calls from compute nodes, that's Private/Dedicated only. I'll add this to the reply.

Ben Carter: Quick clarification: Azure Key Vault direct calls from our hosted compute are not supported. Hosted supports envelope encryption (customer wraps DEK). Dedicated/Private can do direct Azure/GCP/AWS calls.

title: #sales: 1750504321
preview:
Olivia Park: Heads-up — large enterprise prospect wants definitive breach notification timelines and will only accept a flat 72-hour contractual clause. Also asked for CAIQ evidence and RCA timing.

Ben Carter: We should avoid an unconditional 72h promise. Operationally we target initial customer notification within...
text:
Olivia Park: Heads-up — large enterprise prospect wants definitive breach notification timelines and will only accept a flat 72-hour contractual clause. Also asked for CAIQ evidence and RCA timing.

Ben Carter: We should avoid an unconditional 72h promise. Operationally we target initial customer notification within 72 hours of confirmed incident when practicable. Important to distinguish 'detection' vs 'confirmation'.

Mei Wong: Agreed. Suggest wording: "Upon confirmation of a Security Incident materially affecting Customer Data, Redwood will use commercially reasonable efforts to provide Customer with an initial notification within seventy-two (72) hours of confirmation where practicable. Notification timing may be delayed for law enforcement or forensic actions; Redwood will notify Customer if such constraints apply."

Legal Bot: +1. Add required content: severity, affected systems...

Markus Klein: from SE perspective, for true enterprise (VPC/Private/Dedicated) the best CTA is “15-min sizing + workload fit consult” (not generic demo). gets us to: region, concurrency, ctx length, p95 SLO, data residency.

Markus Klein: also recommend we *always* include the methodology link so it doesn’t feel like marketing fluff. prospects ask “what was the workload?” within 1 reply.

- Contacted (two-way interaction: reply, call connect, or Slack/intro)

Lukas Meyer: yep, but with the “preserve existing owner” guardrail. If there’s already an AE owner in HubSpot (or active opp), we keep it and add me as fol...
text:
Stephanie Nguyen: quick confirm for pilot v1 — when we get EU data residency signals, are we routing to EMEA ownership even if the org started self-serve in NA?

Lukas Meyer: yep, but with the “preserve existing owner” guardrail. If there’s already an AE owner in HubSpot (or active opp), we keep it and add me as follower / tag EMEA for assist. If no owner, route to EMEA queue.

Isabelle Laurent: +1. Rules we agreed in ops sync:
1) If `hubspot.company.owner_id` exists AND `lifecycle_stage` >= SQL OR `open_deals_count` > 0 → preserve owner.
2) Else if signal type == `DATA_RESIDENCY_EU` OR `SECURITY_COMPLIANCE_EU` → route to EMEA round-robin.
3) Else default routing by region (billing country / time zone heuristic).

David Kim: implementation detail: we’re setting `plg_routing_region = EMEA` when the signal is one of the EU residency intents. Workflow then assigns from the EMEA rotation ...

Laura Bennett: yep. CFO decision (for ABM pilot / regulated evals):
- We can offer *evaluation credits* (not “free month”) to support a time-boxed pilot.
- Default cap: **up to $15k in usage credits** for Hosted/Dedicated inference usage, **30 days**, expires in **60 days**.
- Credits apply to *usage only* (tokens/embeddings), not to platform fees, support add-ons, or o...
